title: podman-2.1.X build does not preserve the UID & the GID
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

podman build does not preserve the UID & the GID.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a simple Dockerfile

```
FROM busybox
LABEL maintainer Tot <toto@toto.loc>

ADD rootfs.tar /
```

```
mkdir etc
touch etc/test.conf
chmod 600 etc/test.conf
chown 999:999 etc/test.conf
```

```
ls -ali etc/
-rw------- 1  999  999  0 Sep 28 09:17 test.conf
```

```
tar -cvpf rootfs.tar etc
podman build -t test:latest .
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
podman run --name test -ti test:latest sh
ls -ali /etc/test.conf
-rw------- 1  root  root  0 Sep 28 09:17 /etc/test.conf
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
podman run --name test -ti test:latest sh
ls -ali /etc/test.conf
-rw------- 1  999  999  0 Sep 28 09:17 /etc/test.conf
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Tested with podman-2.1.0 and podman-2.1.1

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.16.1
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon'
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.20, commit: '
  cpus: 4
  distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: rakis
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 5.4.0-1025-aws
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 14076997632
  memTotal: 16789577728
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun'
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version UNKNOWN
      commit: 3e46dd849fdf6bfa68127786e073318184641f05
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 85h 39m 42.49s (Approximately 3.54 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 2
    paused: 0
    running: 1
    stopped: 1
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /vol/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: btrfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 80
  runRoot: /run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /vol/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 2.0.0
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.15.2
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.1.1
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

http://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_18.04/

```
podman/unknown,now 2.1.1~1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

AWS EC2 instance.

comments.body:
@babykart were you running the container from root or from a rootless user?
You probably know, but you could add `RUN chmod 600 /etc/test.conf` and `RUN chown 999:999 /etc/test.conf
` to your Dockerfile, but that's not optimal.

Thanks for the report.

@TomSweeneyRedHat I am running podman as root user.
Yes, I know the workaround with RUN but I think it's a little regression since version 2.1.0 because it works as expected with podman-2.0.6. :)
Thanks for the info @babykart and yep, needs to be fixed, just wanted to offer the potential workaround until it is fixed.

@nalind PTAL
This blows up on current buildah as well.
